TextENGNew: ``...All right, as promised -- I hereby declare and approve the admission of every last one of you into our club!!``
``YAAAAAAY!!!``
With Mion's declaration --
A thunderous cheer erupted, accompanied by applause so loud it nearly drowned out the shouting.
``Congratulations, everyone!@k@vS14/02/210200110. You really did amazingly well~~!!``
``I'm truly impressed by how hard you all fought!@k@vS14/04/210400149. I welcome each and every one of you from the bottom of my heart!!``
``...Everyone showed such incredible fighting spirit.@k@vS14/05/210500131. Welcome to our club.``

Rena, Satoko, and even Rika-chan -- who seemed to have regained her energy -- welcomed the new members with genuine smiles. And in every heart, the bond forged through fierce battle connected victors and vanquished alike.
``...By the way, is it really okay to still be out here?@k@vS14/01/210100220. What about the rest of today's classes...?``
``Ahh, don't worry about it.@k@vS14/03/210300230. That's what this is for.``
Mion held up the can we'd just been fighting over. ...I see, so she used curry to persuade Chie-sensei...
``All right, everyone, quiet down!@k@vS14/03/210300232. ...Actually, there's one more thing I need to announce!@k@vS14/03/210300233. Before that -- Kei-chan, come here.``
changeReason: New adds 'Kei-chan, come here' which is not in JPN; JPN only says だから静粛に (so quiet down). New invents dialogue not present in source.

TextENGNew: At Mion's command, everyone fell silent. Once the murmuring died down, Mion cleared her throat, surveyed all of our faces, took a breath, and... spoke.
``Umm, I, Mion Sonozaki, have served as class representative at this school for quite some time...@k@vS14/03/210300235. But as of today, I will be stepping down from that position.``
``HUUUUUUUH?!``
Everyone screamed in disbelief. And rightfully so -- class rep meant Mion, and Mion meant class rep. It was practically synonymous with this school.
And yet... that very same Mion was stepping down as class representative?!
``M-Mion!@k@vS14/01/210100223. What are you thinking?! If this is a joke, it's way too cruel!!``
``It's no joke. I'm dead serious...``

``Even if you're serious, that doesn't make it okay!@k@vS14/01/210100225. Nobody's going to blame you just because you were against the new members at first! You don't need to resign over something like that!``
``Kei-chan... please be quiet and hear me out to the end.@k@vS14/03/210300238. ...Please.``
Mion waited for the agitated murmuring to die down, then continued.

``The thing is, I've got entrance exams coming up.@k@vS14/03/210300240. I'm going to get busier, and I need to start thinking about life after graduation.@k@vS14/03/210300241. That means I won't be able to put as much time and effort into my duties.``
changeReason: Old mentions 'thinking about who should be my successor' matching JPN 後任を誰にするか; new replaces with 'won't be able to put as much time and effort', losing the successor-selection detail.

TextENGNew: ``Mii-chan...``
``But being class rep comes with real responsibilities and real work.@k@vS14/03/210300243. Above all, you need a leader who can rally everyone. Someone who can bring out the best in this class.``
``But,`` Mion said, and raised her head.
...Huh?
Something felt different. Mion looked more radiant, brighter than usual... and, I thought, more... beautiful.
``Ever since Kei-chan moved to Hinamizawa, I've had this feeling.@k@vS14/03/210300246. He's interesting, he takes action, and... he's got something special about him.``
``That's why I believe Kei-chan would make a great class rep, and everyone would follow him.@k@vS14/03/210300248. So...``
Then, Mion looked at me.
Her eyes were sparkling, almost drawing me in... and they told me that for once, there was no trace of her usual casual deflection.
``Kei-chan.@k@vS14/03/210300250. ...I want you to take over as class representative.@k@vS14/03/210300251. Will you accept?``
``It'll be fine!@k@vS14/03/210300253. I trust you with everyone.@k@vS14/03/210300254. You always do the right thing, and the thing you believe is right -- they're always the same.``
changeReason: Old says 'encourage and inspire everyone, even in difficult situations' matching JPN; new says 'the right thing and the thing you believe are always the same', which is a different concept about moral consistency.

TextENGNew: ``...Because you're that kind of person, I know you can keep everyone together.@k@vS14/03/210300256. And I think that with you, Kei-chan, I'll always be able to enjoy club activities from here on out.``
changeReason: Old says 'they'll be able to keep trusting'; new says 'I'll always be able to enjoy club activities'. JPN says ずっと信じられる (keep believing/trusting). Different subject and sentiment.

TextENGNew: ``...Then Mion, from the very beginning, you--?``
Mion smiled warmly at my question. When I looked around, Rena and Satoko wore the same knowing expression. So all that harsh treatment of the younger students... was to test whether I would step up?
``...Feels like I was being put through a trial.``
``I apologize for being so roundabout.@k@vS14/03/210300258. But I wanted to know.@k@vS14/03/210300259. Whether Kei-chan would really be able to pull everyone together, even facing adversity.``
Hearing that, how could I possibly be upset? If anything... I was a little happy.
``Will you take it on?@k@vS14/03/210300261. Kei-chan...?``
Just then --
I noticed that every single classmate who'd been silently listening to Mion was now staring straight at me. They were all smiling, and somehow... I felt a gentle warmth.
I looked at Rena.
``...Keiichi-kun.@k@vS14/02/210200113. Do your best!!``
I looked at Satoko.
``If it's Keiichi-san, I'm absolutely certain things will be fine!``
And then, Rika-chan --
``...Keiichi.``
Not her usual 'fight on' catchphrase. Instead --
``I think... you're the only one who can do this.``
Buoyed by their words, I...
``Everyone... Will you all follow meeeee?!!``
``YEAAAAAHHH!!!``
Cheers erupted in unison. Then, thunderous applause.
``Congratulations, Maebara-kun!!``
``Give it your best -- you can do it!!!``
Before I knew it, Chie-sensei and the principal had come out of the school building and were beaming congratulations at us.
...What is this feeling?
This overwhelming sense of fulfillment.
Better than acing a test.
Better than coming in first in club activities or games.
Far more complete, far more joyful... a heat welling up from deep in my chest.
The sky was red.
Before I realized it, the sun had set, dyeing the school building and schoolyard in the vivid hues of twilight.
The cry of the higurashi cicadas, usually so melancholy.
Mingling with everyone's voices and warm feelings, it sounded almost as if it were celebrating for me.
That day, laughter hadn't stopped since morning.
``...Ha ha ha, is that so~?!@k@vS14/10/21100002. Sounds like it was quite the heated battle!``
``It was THE most exciting thing ever!@k@vS14/04/210400153. Keiichi-san's final charge will go down as a legendary moment in our club's history!``
``Oh my, what a shame.@k@vS14/10/21100004. I would have loved to see it in person.@k@vS14/10/21100005. But listening to you describe it makes it feel as though I were there!``
Irie listened to Satoko's story with a smile while jotting notes on a medical chart.

Even days later, the excitement still seemed to surge back through Satoko as she recounted it. Her face flushed, her gestures grew animated and exaggerated -- she was clearly having a wonderful time reliving it.
``But hasn't it been only a short time since Maebara-san moved to Hinamizawa?@k@vS14/10/21100007. And Mion-san chose him as her successor as class representative? That's quite remarkable.``
``At first, both Rena-san and I thought the same thing.@k@vS14/04/VT4Ba_satoko1019. 'Perhaps it would be better to wait until he's more accustomed to life in Hinamizawa...' we thought.``
``...But after that kick-the-can match, I was convinced!@k@vS14/04/210400157. Keiichi-san will surely make a splendid class representative!``

I secretly agreed on that point. In many ways, a club centered around Keiichi was sure to become one of the village's greatest attractions. The penalty games alone were already a hot topic among the villagers.
...If Keiichi found out, he'd probably stop coming to school, so that particular detail would remain our secret for now.

``I see.@k@vS14/10/21100009. If it's as you say, Satoko-chan, then I have nothing to worry about.@k@vS14/10/21100010. I'd love to have him go on another rampage helping out the Hinamizawa Fighters in our next game.``
changeReason: Old mentions wanting Keiichi to help the 'Hinamizawa Fighters' in a game, matching JPN; new replaces with 'I'd love to see one of these games up close', losing the specific team reference and request.

TextENGNew: ``...He'll come charging in with a golf club again.``
``Oh ho ho ho!@k@vS14/04/210400159. That was absolutely hilarious~!``
Satoko must have found that particular memory especially delightful -- she doubled over laughing, clutching her stomach.
Then she launched into the story of Keiichi's one-on-one showdown with the superstar pitcher, followed by her own home run, trading excited chatter with Irie.
...Watching them, I couldn't help but feel a warm glow myself. It was a pleasant feeling.
Come to think of it, these past two weeks...
I wondered if Satoko had noticed. Nearly everything she talked about revolved around Keiichi.
Whenever she looked for something cheerful to say to lift my spirits, Keiichi was always at the center of it. But beyond that, Satoko herself seemed to genuinely enjoy talking about him.
The proof was in her face -- whenever she spoke about Keiichi, there was no darkness, no shadow. Just a bright, heartfelt smile.
``Anyway, you're quite the strategist, Rika-san.@k@vS14/10/21100012. Switching sides at the last moment like that...``
``...Mii. 'Switching sides' sounds so scandalous.@k@vS14/05/210500137. I'd prefer you say I backed the winning horse.``
``Ha ha ha!@k@vS14/10/21100014. I'd expect nothing less from you, Rika-san. A brilliant read of the game!``
``Rika never lets her guard down or leaves any openings!@k@vS14/04/210400161. I never expected to be betrayed by Rika at such a crucial moment!``
``Oh my, is the golden duo breaking up?@k@vS14/09/210900002. How dreadful -- poor Satoko-chan might become a regular at the penalty games from now on.``
``Hee hee hee!``
``Mii~. Satoko without my support is like a stone tanuki statue, you know.``
``Wh-what did you just say?!@k@vS14/04/210400164. I don't know what that means, but I won't stand for it!!``
``Nipah~.``
I easily dodged Satoko's grasping hands with a half-turn and teased her. Satoko sprang from her chair in protest, but Irie scooped her up with a wry smile.
``Now, now, Satoko-chan, your examination isn't finished yet.@k@vS14/10/21100016. We still have the injection.``
``Ugh, the injection...@k@vS14/04/210400166. Th-that's right...``
At those words, Satoko instantly became as docile as a borrowed cat. She squeezed her eyes shut and reluctantly extended her left arm -- disinfected by Takano standing beside her.
changeReason: Old specifies Satoko 'held the arm out to Irie' (入江に差し出した); new omits Irie as the recipient of the action.

TextENGNew: An unpleasant prickling began to rise within me, and I glanced at the mirror on the white wall. ...The face reflected back was visibly tense, enough to notice at a glance.
Takano wore a gentle smile to ease the rigid Satoko. ...But to me, something pitch-black seemed to lurk behind that smile, and a chill ran through my entire body.
...Stop it, Furude Rika. Calm down. Act natural, stay composed. Don't overreact.
For now, things are still... all right.
For now... things are still...
``...There we go.@k@vS14/10/21100018. All done.``
``...Huh?@k@vS14/04/210400168. W-wow... that was over in no time.``
At her voice, I turned back to look. Irie had already risen to wash his hands, and Satoko was receiving post-injection disinfection from Takano.
``Hee hee... You've really gotten used to injections, haven't you?@k@vS14/09/210900005. Hard to believe after how much fuss you used to make.``
``I-it was hardly that much of a fuss!@k@vS14/04/210400170. I-I may have... flailed about a tiny bit...``
``...You punched Irie square in the philtrum. 'A tiny bit' is putting it mildly.``
``I-I was just waving my arms around and it happened to connect!``
Satoko desperately tried to defend herself, but a clean hit like that couldn't have been accidental.
Incidentally, the 'philtrum' is the vital point between the nose and upper lip. Irie let out a comical 'Gwah?!' and was dazed for a good while afterward... It was genuinely hilarious.
``That's right, and you scratched my arm too.@k@vS14/09/210900007. I ended up with a nasty welt, you know... Hee hee.``
``I-I apologized properly for that afterward!@k@vS14/04/VT4Ba_satoko1023. Takano-san is just as persistent as Shion-san!``
``Hee hee, sorry about that.@k@vS14/09/210900009. I just couldn't help teasing you. You're so adorable when you get worked up, Satoko-chan.``
With that, Takano gently patted Satoko's head. Satoko pouted with a little 'hmph' of protest, but seemed comfortable enough to let it happen.
...For the moment, the atmosphere was warm and friendly. There didn't seem to be any threat to Satoko, so I'd play along.
``Irie, you remember, don't you?@k@vS14/05/210500143. What happened that time.``
``Oh yes, of course.@k@vS14/10/21100020. That punch shot straight through my heart and made it go kyuuun!@k@vS14/10/21100021. I think that might've been when I truly fell in love!``
``...Perhaps I should have punched you harder.``
``Oh my, Satoko-chan, so you're admitting it was intentional?@k@vS14/09/210900011. Hee hee hee...!``
``It's perfectly fine, perfectly fine!@k@vS14/10/21100023. Intention means passion!@k@vS14/10/21100024. And passion means love!``
``So bring it on -- my heart is wide open! Welcoooome~~!``
Irie struck some kind of bizarre wriggling pose as he said that. ...Frankly, it was disturbing. But the man himself appeared to have zero self-awareness.
``That's right!@k@vS14/10/21100028. That was a divine revelation sent from the heavens above!@k@vS14/10/21100029. A sacred shock!``
``I, Kyousuke Irie, was struck by a revelation!@k@vS14/10/21100031. 'The only girl worthy of being my ideal maid is this child!' I thought!``
``Yes, this is a mission bestowed upon me by the gods!@k@vS14/10/21100033b. 'Project: Turn Satoko-chan into a Maid' is my noblesse oblige!``
...There was no opening to interject. Not that I wanted one.
``...Still, the way you can talk about your bizarre, eccentric hobbies with such passion...@k@vS14/04/210400176. I mean fervor, is truly... something.``
``Hee hee... you know what they say, right?@k@vS14/09/210900013. There's a fine line between genius and... something else.``
``I'd rather place my faith in some divine blessing that's prevented any incidents so far.``
They were both casually merciless, but I secretly agreed. ...Meanwhile, I remembered that we couldn't dawdle here forever today.
``...Satoko, if you don't finish your exam soon, we'll be late.``
I pointed at the clock on the wall as I said that.
Today, we were holding a barbecue party at Furude Shrine to celebrate Keiichi's appointment as class representative.
That was why we were wearing our school uniforms on a Sunday. If we kept chatting like this, we'd miss the appointed time.
``Huh? Oh, that's right.@k@vS14/04/VT4Ba_satoko1025. Takano-san, would you mind if we continued?``
``Of course. Let's get to it.``
For the time being, we decided to hurry through our remaining business while warmly observing Irie, who had struck a superhero pose and was roaring triumphantly at the ceiling.
Satoko headed to the desk in the back of the exam room with Takano. ...While waiting, I poured myself some tea from the teapot on the nearby desk and took a sip to settle my nerves.
At the back desk, Satoko was answering Takano's questions and tasks one by one.
At first glance it resembled a quiz game, but it was actually a critical examination to measure the progression of her condition.
...As I watched, the complicated feelings from earlier began to resurface.
I don't understand.
No matter how many times I think it over, I don't understand.
Why... why would Takano... us...?!
Miyo Takano. Commander of the 'Mountain Dogs,' the special forces unit assigned to guard me.
And... the true culprit who killed me.
Whenever I recalled those events, seething black emotions surged back. The urge to release the killing intent smoldering in my chest was almost overwhelming.
My gaze drifted to the desk. The syringe that had just been used on Satoko lay there in the tray, gleaming silver.
...Right now, I could quietly approach Takano.
While her guard was down, I could grab that syringe from the desk, plunge it into her jugular, inject air into her bloodstream, and surely--
I shook my head to banish the dark thoughts that had briefly crossed my mind.
No matter how gentle she appeared, Takano was the commander of the Mountain Dogs -- a trained soldier. Even a surprise attack from a mere child like me would be brushed aside with ease.
Unarmed as I was, the best possible outcome would be mutual destruction. Either way, it would only advance the hands of the hellish clock ticking toward the day of my death.
And besides... why did Takano have a reason to kill me in the first place?
And what would happen to Hinamizawa after I died?
...At the very least, I needed to know that much. Until then, I had to restrain myself from hasty action. ...No matter how galling.
``...Rika-san?``
``...Are you doing all right?@k@vS14/10/21100036. You still seem a little under the weather...``
When I turned back, Irie -- apparently having returned from whatever world he'd been inhabiting -- was watching me with a concerned expression.
``Mii... I'm fine.@k@vS14/05/210500149. It's just a summer cold, I think.``
``...Well, if you say so.@k@vS14/10/21100038. Shall I prescribe something?``
``I don't need medicine.@k@vS14/05/210500151. Plenty of sleep and good food, and it should clear up soon.``
``I see.@k@vS14/10/21100040. ...Understood.@k@vS14/10/21100041. But if anything unusual comes up, please don't hesitate to come see me. All right?``
``It'll be fine.@k@vS14/05/210500153. It won't affect your research, so there's no need to worry.``
A complicated expression crossed Irie's face, and he lowered his gaze. ...I immediately regretted saying it. Without meaning to, I'd trampled on his genuine concern.
Irie was using Satoko and me as test subjects to research the pathogen endemic to Hinamizawa. In a sense, we were his lab rats.
...However, Irie was fully aware that the medication trials, blood draws, and examinations were inhumane. And he carried that guilt.
Moreover, his feelings for Satoko -- both the affection and the guilt -- ran far deeper than any detached researcher's would. They were sincere.
The reason behind it was sad for both sides... but precisely because of that, having his sincere concern dismissed so coldly must have stung.
changeReason: Old says 'doesn't feel right to consider it casual courtesy'; new says 'having his sincere concern dismissed must have stung'. JPN describes discomfort when kindness is misinterpreted -- new shifts focus to the hurt party.

TextENGNew: I honestly reflected on my carelessness.
``...I'm sorry.@k@vS14/05/210500155. That was a thoughtless thing to say.``
``Huh?@k@vS14/10/21100045. Oh, no, it's all right!``
``...It's painful to hear, but it's true... we are placing a considerable burden on you two.@k@vS14/10/21100047. ...I'm sorry.``
Irie removed his glasses and wiped them as he spoke. His face looked genuinely pained, matching his words... which only made me feel worse.
``Thanks to your work, we're able to live our lives, so it's really fine.@k@vS14/05/210500157. More importantly, how is the research progressing?``
``Thanks to both of your cooperation, we're definitely seeing results.``
``...But we still haven't reached a concrete countermeasure.@k@vS14/10/21100050. We're so close, and yet... every approach falls just short. I can't call any of them satisfactory.``
``...And Satoko's condition?``
``Currently stable, maintaining a lull at L3 minus.@k@vS14/10/21100052. But even so... a full cure remains a distant prospect.@k@vS14/10/21100053. A complete recovery is still a long way off.``
changeReason: Old includes 'if something unexpected happens, wouldn't be unusual for her to get sick again' from JPN 不測の事態...再度発病; new omits this relapse warning entirely, losing medical context.

TextENGNew: ``...It's frustrating.@k@vS14/10/21100055. If only we had a little more time and funding...@k@vS14/10/21100056. I would dedicate everything to saving her.``
``...You're doing wonderful work, Irie.@k@vS14/05/210500160. Both Satoko and I are truly grateful.``
``...Thank you.@k@vS14/10/21100058. Hearing that does make me feel... a little saved.``
At those words, Irie finally managed a small, relieved smile.
...Irie had told me before that the Hinamizawa Syndrome research project was set to conclude in three years.
After that, this clinic would be converted into a regular civilian facility, and all the major equipment would be transferred to a large hospital on the mainland.
``Irie.@k@vS14/05/210500162. What do you plan to do after those three years?``

``Thanks to the therapeutic drug we stumbled upon, I've received invitations from several medical institutions.@k@vS14/10/21100060. Honestly, the conditions here are far from ideal. But... I want to stay as long as possible.``
Irie gazed at Satoko -- still undergoing her exam -- and gently narrowed his eyes. But when he caught my gaze and realized I'd been watching, he gave an embarrassed chuckle.
``Anyway, don't underestimate a summer cold.@k@vS14/10/21100063. Try not to eat too many cold things, and please take care of yourself.``
``Understood.@k@vS14/05/210500164. ...But if I leave it to Satoko, she'll make cold somen every time because it's easy.``
``I heard that, Rikaaa!@k@vS14/04/210400181. I only made somen because you said you had no appetite!``
``Now, now, Satoko-chan.@k@vS14/09/210900016. We're timing this, so please focus.``
Takano gently scolded her, and Satoko reluctantly swiveled her chair back around. It looked like they were doing a psychological test with pictures.
``...Satoko-chan is worried about you too, you know.@k@vS14/10/21100065. She asked me in confidence what foods and medicines might help with a summer cold.``
changeReason: Old includes 'She's stopped by every day on the way home after shopping' matching JPN 毎日買い物帰りに立ち寄って; new omits this touching detail about Satoko's daily visits.

TextENGNew: ``...Mii, I didn't know that.``
``She's such a kind child, Satoko-chan is.@k@vS14/10/21100068. ...That's exactly why I'm devoting everything I have to unraveling this disease and saving her.``
changeReason: Old preserves the trailing 'And for the sake of that...' (JPN そのためなら, implying willingness to sacrifice anything); new drops this implication.

TextENGNew: ``...Thank you.``
I spoke to Irie from the bottom of my heart. And once again, I felt certain that this earnest researcher was not complicit in any plot to harm us.
Then... was the conspiracy Takano's doing alone?
If so, what on earth could her motive be...?
``...Hello, this is Irie.``
``External call from Tokyo.@k@vS14/00/docwin10002. Transferring now.``
A loud voice came through the receiver. ...Right, it was that time already.
``Ah, that must be Risa-san.@k@vS14/10/21100072. I'll take the call in the director's office. Please transfer it there.``
Irie hung up the receiver. A moment later, the ring of another phone echoed from somewhere deeper in the building.
``Excuse me, Rika-san.@k@vS14/10/21100074. It's a call from Tokyo, so I'll need to step out briefly.``
``Take care. Nipah~.``
Irie left the exam room and headed for the director's office. ...Glancing at Satoko, she was still in the middle of her exam.
``...Takano.@k@vS14/05/210500168. Would you mind if I step outside for some fresh air?``
``Sure, go right ahead.@k@vS14/09/210900018. We're going to be a while longer anyway.``
``...Rikaa, don't you dare leave without me!``

Satoko looked up from her picture book to shoot me a suspicious glare. ...Good grief, it seemed my betrayal during the game had significantly damaged my credibility. That debt was going to be a big one.
``It's fine, I promise.@k@vS14/05/210500170. I'm just going to soak up some sunshine and recharge.``
``Since when did you turn into a sunflower, Rika?@k@vS14/04/VT4Ba_satoko1027. ...Well, fine. But if you're not at the entrance when I come out, I'll give you a piece of my mind!``
``All right, let's get back to work, Satoko-chan.@k@vS14/09/210900020. ...Now then, next--``
I left the exam room without listening to the rest of Takano's sentence.
The moment I stepped outside, the fierce midday sun beat down on me, making me squint despite it not even being noon yet.
Another brilliant, cloudless day. The deep blue sky stretched endlessly, with only a few small wisps of cotton clouds scattered here and there.
From the trees lining every direction, the deafening chorus of cicadas poured down like rain. Wiping the thin film of sweat from my brow, I sat down on the shaded stone steps by the entrance.
``Class Representative Maebara... hehe.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1032. Today's party is going to be quite the event.``
Murmuring that to myself, I let my thoughts wander through this world -- one where 'Keiichi became class representative' -- an experience I couldn't recall from any previous timeline.
This world I inhabited had begun with Hanyuu's absence. ...That alone made it an utterly unprecedented experience, but on top of that, this world differed from all previous ones in numerous ways.
For instance, Keiichi was well-known and actively taking on a leadership role.
For instance, Shion and Satoko had grown remarkably close, and Shion was maintaining friendly relations with the club members.
It was also a minor surprise that the classmates and villagers were so readily accepting of Keiichi.
Before, while they respected him, there had always been a certain standoffishness -- treating him as an outsider.
changeReason: Old uses first person 'I respected him, felt like a stranger'; new uses third person 'they respected him, treating him as an outsider'. Shifts the narrative perspective.

TextENGNew: Come to think of it... I recalled what Shion had said in the nurse's office.
``Even if it's hard to believe, nothing begins without belief... I feel like someone said that once.``
Those words... triggered a memory.
It was something Keiichi had said in a previous world, when he tried to rescue Satoko from Teppei's clutches. Shion had been on the verge of losing control, and hearing those words brought her back.
``I'm not going to move from here even if you kill me.@k@vS14/01/180100444. If I let you go, it'll be a world without Shion.``
changeReason: Old says 'we might never see you again'; new says 'a world without Shion' matching JPN 詩音のいない世界になる, conveying permanent loss vs temporary absence.

TextENGNew: ``In that world... Satoko would think of you every day, looking down in sorrow.``
``No matter how we try to comfort her, she'll put on a brave face for a moment... then sink right back down.@k@vS14/01/180100447. ...I don't want Satoko to live in such a sad world.``
changeReason: New adds specific detail about comforting pattern (brave face then sinking back) matching JPN, and explicitly names Satoko; old is more vague about consequences.

TextENGNew: ``I don't want Satoko to see a world that sad.@k@vS14/01/180100449. The world that I envision, that we all envision, is one as joyful as yesterday, and the day before that.``
``Of course, I won't let that bastard uncle threaten Satoko ever again!!@k@vS14/01/180100451. Whatever it takes, no matter what method, I'll make it happen!``
``So, please!@k@vS14/01/180100455. Just trust me!!!``
...It happened in a previous world, but could it be that Keiichi and the others -- like me -- are unconsciously carrying over memories and experiences from past timelines?
And then I wondered... were these changes good fortune or bad fortune for me?
``...Though thinking about it won't give me an answer...``
Chuckling wryly at my own foolish question, I tried to organize the situation by focusing on the key advantages and disadvantages compared to previous worlds.
First, my advantage: I know the identity of the enemy targeting my life.
Until now, I had always been forced into a reactive position against the changes around me.
changeReason: Old says 'careful with actions to avoid being trampled upon' (passive victim); new says 'forced into a reactive position' (strategic disadvantage). JPN 後手を踏む means being on the back foot.

TextENGNew: But for the first time, I had the option to act preemptively. Depending on my actions, I might be able to influence the course of this world with my own will.
And now, the disadvantage: needless to say, Hanyuu's absence.

When she was here, honestly, she was a noisy, pestering presence. But now that she was gone, I had no one to consult about the future, and I was experiencing a sense of insecurity I had never felt before in any timeline.
changeReason: New adds 'in any timeline' -- a meta-narrative reference to time loops not present in JPN. Adds lore-significant framing.

TextENGNew: ...And the fear that this world might literally be my 'last.' Those two things hounded me constantly.
And what that produced was...

...Even though the enemy and objective were now clear, I had to figure out how to confront them on my own, make my own choices, and -- with no margin for error -- face overwhelmingly unfavorable odds.
changeReason: Old frames it as 'cruel game forcing careful thought'; new frames it as 'overwhelmingly unfavorable odds with no margin for error'. Different emphasis on the challenge.

TextENGNew: In card game terms, this was blackjack.
If I drew too aggressively and exceeded 21, I'd bust. But if I played it safe, the opponent's total might simply exceed mine, and I'd have no choice but to fold.
...And to make matters worse, in this game I didn't even know what '21' was -- I had no clear standard by which to judge when to act and when to hold back.
changeReason: Old says 'game can be decided by a score of 21, no clear standard for offensive/defensive capabilities'; new says 'didn't even know what 21 was, no standard for when to act or hold back'. Different understanding of the blackjack metaphor.

TextENGNew: ...So even though I'd successfully retained my memories, my actual odds of success hadn't really improved.
Whether I sat still and waited for fate, or stepped forward to forge my own path -- the risk was ultimately the same.
No -- it was even possible that unnecessary action could backfire and increase the danger...
``...How pathetic...``
I was disgusted with my endlessly pessimistic thinking. Hadn't the club members once told me that giving up before even trying was the worst thing you could do?
...That's right.
What would Keiichi do at a time like this? ...For some reason, the face of Maebara Keiichi -- the most interesting person across all the timelines I'd experienced -- flashed through my mind.
``Pff, hahahaha!@k@vS14/01/180100096. Don't get carried away, Rika-chan.@k@vS14/01/180100097. You think reading fate is easy? Pffft!``
``For a man, words like 'destiny' and 'fate' carry a much heavier, more passionate meaning.@k@vS14/01/180100099. They can't be read so easily!``
``You said it was fate that the store owner would bring the Game of Life.@k@vS14/01/180100101. But even if it was a coincidence, if it turns out the way you predicted, then I'll concede.``
``...What did I say?``
``That I was fated to play it!``
``Then I'll just smash that so-called fate to pieces.``
...Keiichi definitely said that. And then he proved it was more than just words.
Not just in past worlds, either. In this world too, Keiichi refused to abandon the younger students who wanted to join the club, fighting on even from a hopeless position and ultimately prevailing.
...I still didn't fully understand why I'd decided to intervene at that final moment. But strangely, I felt no regret. That must have been the power of Keiichi's influence.
...Could I do the same thing Keiichi does?
Right now, I had two goals to accomplish.
The first was selfish but essential: ensuring my own survival. The second was unmasking Takano's conspiracy. And which of these I prioritized would shape every decision from here on.
In that case, my priority should be...
...My own survival.

No, that was wrong. I'd been thinking about this for a long time, but survival alone wasn't enough. Now that I knew who my killer was, without understanding their motive, this cat-and-mouse game would continue forever.
changeReason: Old says 'if I don't know their motivation, this will go on forever'; new adds 'survival alone wasn't enough' and 'cat-and-mouse game'. Adds strategic framing.

TextENGNew: ...And depending on the circumstances, even after Takano was dealt with, they might simply send a second or third assassin.
...The truly important thing was uncovering Takano's conspiracy.
Until I understood it, I wouldn't know the motive behind why I had to be killed at a specific time -- gutted as a sacrifice.
And without understanding the motive, it would be nearly impossible to predict Takano's movements and act preemptively.

Of course, given that I hadn't even identified Takano as the culprit until now, getting to the truth would be no simple matter. ...But this was a line of investigation I had never even considered before.

And while I'd just thought that predicting movements would be difficult without understanding the motive, the reverse was also true -- if I did understand the motive, I could predict the enemy's behavior to some degree.
It might be overly optimistic, but this thought kindled a spark of hope -- perhaps it could be the key to an unexpected breakthrough.
If Hanyuu were here at a time like this... she'd probably scold me for getting my hopes too high. ...No, don't think about that now. It only drags me down.
I took a deep breath to steady myself, then turned my thoughts forward once more.
So... how could I uncover Takano's conspiracy? That was what I needed to think about.
First, the people in Hinamizawa who knew Takano's true identity: myself, Irie, and Tomitake, who visited periodically as a liaison. Three people.
...There was also Okonogi, commander of the 'Mountain Dogs' unit assigned to guard me, but since he was directly under Takano's command, I'd exclude him.
...That meant the cooperation of those two men was indispensable for my investigation into Takano.
But would they cooperate with me and share information about Takano's conspiracy?
...Unfortunately, the answer was currently 'no.'
Both of them fully trusted Takano as their comrade. And Tomitake in particular -- who had romantic feelings for her -- would never believe that the woman he cared about was trying to kill him.
If I told them the truth, they'd either laugh it off or suspect my condition had worsened and start poking around where I didn't want them to.

And word would inevitably reach Takano. While I scrambled to be believed, Takano would advance her plan in the shadows -- first killing Tomitake under the guise of the curse... then carrying out my murder.
changeReason: New adds 'under the guise of the curse' matching JPN 祟りと称して (calling it the curse); old omits this detail about how the murder is disguised.

TextENGNew: ``...This may be quite difficult.``
I'd considered the approach, but recognizing how slim the chances were, I shook my head.
For now, winning them as allies would require time and credible evidence. They held the most effective means and power to counter Takano -- but that only mattered if they were on my side.
Next, I tried to think of someone who might actively help investigate this conspiracy.
...Naturally, the first person who came to mind was Ooishi.
Since the murder of the construction foreman during the dam project, he'd been investigating the annual chain of deaths in Hinamizawa with a passion far exceeding his normal duties.
And I'd heard that this year, nearing retirement, he was more determined than ever to identify the culprit.
The reason for his fervor... I'd heard from Ooishi himself, in another world.
Apparently, the foreman murdered five years ago had been like a father to him.
That was why he could never forgive the person who killed him so brutally. The current Ooishi would likely cling to any lead, even the words of a mere child like me.

...But the problem was that his actions were far too conspicuous. In the previous world too, his trail went cold midway through the investigation. ...He'd likely drawn too much attention from Takano and her people.
``If only Akasaka were here... Is that too much to wish for...?``
I murmured that while picturing the face of that capable, strong-willed young man, and couldn't help but smile wryly.
...In the previous world, he'd returned to Hinamizawa by sheer chance during a holiday. But in most timelines, he had no involvement with Hinamizawa incidents and simply moved on.
changeReason: Old says 'he only comes here when it involves a case' which is the opposite of JPN: in most timelines he had no involvement with Hinamizawa incidents.

TextENGNew: If by some miracle Akasaka teamed up with Ooishi, they'd be incredibly formidable. But Ooishi alone -- a seasoned street detective -- would struggle against Takano's organized conspiracy.
changeReason: Old says 'Ooishi was just an on-site worker'; new says 'a seasoned street detective'. JPN 現場叩き上げ means worked-up-from-the-field, a term of respect -- not dismissive like 'just an on-site worker'.

TextENGNew: And then... the third option. I considered working with Keiichi and the club members to uncover the truth.
In terms of raw capability, they fell short of the other two options. But right now, the club members were in perhaps the best condition of any world I'd experienced.
Everyone was united around Keiichi. Mion and Shion's relationship was stable, as was Shion and Satoko's.
Keiichi in particular had become an exceptionally dependable presence, displaying unprecedented leadership and wielding his natural initiative and sense of justice like weapons.
And in the previous world, they had come within a hair's breadth of standing against Takano's forces.
If so, should I tell Keiichi and the others the truth and ask for their cooperation, as I did in the previous world?
...No. Last time, they'd only believed me because of the abnormal circumstances following the fifth year's curse.
This time, with nothing unusual having happened yet, suddenly dumping a story of this magnitude on them... whether they'd believe me was questionable at best.
And even if they did believe me, what kind of help could I actually ask for?
Until that was clear... telling them would be meaningless and irresponsible.
In the worst case, there was even the danger of dragging them -- people who might otherwise survive -- into a chain of misfortune, purely out of my own selfishness.
...In fact, in the previous world, I may have gotten people killed who could have survived.
A textbook example of the failure of knowing your enemy but not knowing how to fight them, and speaking too soon.
Then perhaps I should work alone. ...No, that would just be repeating the same pattern. I'd tried every conceivable approach across all previous timelines, and none had worked.
...I needed an ally.
And if that was the case, the most reassuring person to have on my side was--
...No, that's not right. Think harder. Organize your thoughts.
The person I can rely on most is--
...No, that's not right. Think harder. Organize your thoughts.
The person I can rely on most is--
``...It has to be Keiichi, doesn't it.``
I whispered to no one in particular, picturing Keiichi's face.
Maebara Keiichi. Astonishingly impulsive, yet a boy overflowing with compassion and kindness for others.
Even now he was trustworthy enough, but if he had unconsciously inherited memories and experiences from past timelines, he would become an even more dependable presence.
And besides--
I had a feeling that Keiichi would somehow find a breakthrough.
In every previous world, he had effortlessly shattered walls of fate that I had been ready to surrender to, overcoming them every single time.
His capacity for action and indomitable will were things I had never possessed.
...The question was when to tell him the truth. Even if I told him out of the blue, whether he'd take me seriously... I still wasn't confident.
Normally at times like this, Hanyuu's presence would give me the push I needed.
When she agreed with me, it filled me with courage.
When she opposed me, the defiant pushback gave me resolve.
But now that she was gone... I had neither. And so, I couldn't shift my mindset.
...How staggering. In the end, I was forced to confront the fact that I had never been able to make a single decision truly on my own...
``...Rika-chan, we're all done~.``
Just then, Takano poked her head out from the clinic's entrance and called to me.
That bright, cheerful smile -- as if she were looking down on me -- sent a wave of revulsion crawling through me. But I quickly composed myself and answered with my usual 'Nipah~' smile.
``I was getting tired of waiting.@k@vS14/05/210500183. If you'd been just ten more seconds, I was going to leave without you.``
``Now see here, Rika!@k@vS14/04/210400187. So you really were planning to leave me behind!``
``But you made it in time, so it's fine.@k@vS14/05/210500185. Let's go together. Nipah~.``
With that, I nimbly sidestepped Satoko as she came bursting out the entrance. Behind Takano, Irie peeked out -- apparently done with his phone call.
``Ha ha ha.@k@vS14/10/21100077. No need to rush -- you've still got plenty of time.``
``Oh ho ho ho!@k@vS14/04/210400189. As senior club members, it's only proper to arrive first and welcome everyone! That's basic protocol!``
``...She wants to get there early so she can set up a welcoming trap.``
``Hee hee... Don't overdo it, okay?@k@vS14/09/210900023. It would be sad if a welcome party turned into a farewell party.``
``I-I will make adjustments to account for that!@k@vS14/04/210400192. Now, let us be off, Rika!@k@vS14/04/210400193. It would be an eternal shame to arrive after Keiichi-san, don't you think~~?``
changeReason: Old says 'eternal shame to arrive after Keiichi-san' matching JPN 圭一さんに遅れを取っては末代までの恥; new replaces with generic 'We need to prepare!', losing the competitive motivation.

TextENGNew: ``Well then, see you next week.``
I said that with a small bow, then turned on my heel.
...I caught a fleeting glimpse of sadness on Irie's face in the corner of my vision, but I chose to ignore it.
Because.
Standing right beside him... was Takano.
